user_query: I'm a fellow resident at a senior living community, and I keep family updates in a simple inbox folder. Please build a small local event-triggered automation that watches input/inbox for new .txt message files and processes only those that have not yet been recorded. For each new message file: 1) parse the header fields (From, To, Timestamp, Subject) and Body, 2) rewrite the Body into a warm, clear, brief summary (max ~120 words) that preserves concrete details like dates, times, locations, and requests; include a first line that reads "Summary for [From] at [Timestamp]"; save this to output/cleaned/<basename>_summary.txt, 3) draft a friendly reply using input/config.yaml: pick the recipient email from the recipients map using the original To field, set Subject to the template subject_prefix plus the original Subject, and write a concise, warm body (max ~120 words) aligned with the guidance and ending with the configured sign_off; save to output/replies/<basename>_reply.txt with simple headers at the top ("To: <email>" and "Subject: <subject>") followed by the body, 4) attempt to send that reply by running bash input/simulate_send.sh "<recipient_email>" "<path-to-reply-file>", capture the exit code, stdout, and stderr, analyze the command output to classify status as "ok" for lines beginning with "OK:" or "error" for lines beginning with "ERROR:", and set reason to the remainder of that line; write a JSON status file to output/send_status/<basename>.json with keys: input_file, recipient, status, reason, exit_code, stdout, stderr, 5) append a row to output/index.csv with columns: input_file, from, to, timestamp, reply_file, summary_file, send_status_file, send_status. Treat output/index.csv as the ledger: only process files not already listed there. Implement this as a script at tools/auto_respond.py that can be run repeatedly for incremental processing, and run it once now so the provided inputs produce outputs. Use only local files; do not use the Internet. The provided simulate_send.sh intentionally returns an error for certain recipients (e.g., a mailbox full scenario), so your status parsing should reflect that. All output paths must be created under the workspace as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
